All beeches should be public access. Who the hell do these people think they are??

Last summer, a 40ft timber fence appeared at one end of what has always been regarded as a public beach on Sandbanks, Poole, Dorset. The 'eyesore' structure was accompanied by officious signage stating 'private beach' and 'trespassers will be prosecuted' along with a security camera with motion sensors. The so-called 'land grab' by a millionaire insurance tycoon and his wife along Sandbanks' northern shore in Dorset put a stop to more than 80 years of public access to the beach there. Although a council official has previously said that the sand in dispute is a private beach, angry residents clubbed together to hire a top planning barrister to look into whether the foreshore was indeed public or private.
  • 605 comments

  • I was 43 when I lost my beloved husband. He is now 36yrs dead. We had 3 beautiful daughters ages 11-7 - 5. They were our love and the best thing we’d ever done. They’re all grown up now with their own wonderful husbands and families. It’s only now in the past 15yrs I feel the loneliness. I’d hate to be a burden to my girls or their partners. I kept myself very busy working and rearing my girls. The only regret I may have is not going out and look for another partner but I always felt no one could match the one true love of my life who was no gone. Everyone thought I was happy in my own world. Little do they know I’m heartbroken - even 36yrs on - So advice - if someone comes along who’s even 75%as nice as you darling husband was - give yourself and him a chance. Don’t rush in but don’t be on your own for the rest of your days. The first 10yrs are the hardest. It’s all a blur but after that - be your own person. Sorry for your

At least five men in the Mexican state of Jalisco have been tightly bound to lampposts across the last two weeks after being suspected of stealing the vehicles. The men, many of whom had their mouths taped shut, were found with the word 'ratero' - Spanish for 'thief' - inked onto their foreheads.

Killing a young woman is not in the old school code.

No matter how Taylor, now 64, glossed over his criminality - attacking other thugs he deemed to be below him, such as paedophiles, was a lifelong speciality - the truth was much darker. Just how evil this hulking 6ft 2in 'psycho' really is finally emerged last week when he was handed a whole life term for the callous murder of a vulnerable young woman whose family are pleading for Taylor to reveal what he did with her body. His role in the crime could only be disclosed after he was convicted of murdering child killer Kyle Bevan in his prison cell in November last year, while being held on remand at HMP Wakefield for the young woman's murder. Taylor and two other convicts already serving whole-life sentences - double killer Lee Newell and gangland assassin Mark Fellows - stabbed Bevan 25 times and left him to bleed out in his cell. Today, for the first time, we can paint a full picture of David Alec Taylor, who has led a life of crime for almost half-a-century but had previously managed to stay under the radar.
  • 9 comments
  • 1 video
  • share Meet David Taylor the self-proclaimed 'old-fashioned villain'

In ten years you can't afford any of the extras because its gone up so much, sell your house and you are worse off. Millions of people will never be able to buy. Yet housing is allocated for migrants and the poor - why can't the same be done for those in the middle who can't afford to buy?

Parker Roll, 12, was swimming with his older brother Jack, 16, after the two decided to take a dip while on a boat tour of the Exuma Cays when he was bitten.

So under Leibore-nomics it's better to have an empty building contributing Nothing to the economy AND still No VAT being generated, than a school that was actually teaching and employing people. I have taught Economics and guess what mark I'd give you, Labour.

Tucked away in the affluent, leafy Farnham Lane, just north of Haslemere town centre in Surrey, lies a vacant £8 million property. With 15 buildings, including a sports centre equipped with two netball courts, situated on 24.36 acres of rolling land, one might assume prospective homeowners are flocking for a tour.
